_comment: SkillOpt build benchmark. Each task is built with the candidate skill, then scored: renders without errors? required elements/text present? AND (where declared) does the app actually WORK when driven? Interactions are driven by visible text/role and mostly assert that body.innerText changed, so they can't be satisfied by a static mockup and don't depend on the agent's CSS class names. train = optimize on; val = the held-out gate.

train.prompt: Build a centered 'Hello, World' landing page with a gradient background, a big heading, and a single call-to-action button. Single index.html. Then call done.
Build a counter app: a large number starting at 0, with a + button and a - button that increase and decrease it. Make it work with vanilla JavaScript. Single index.html. Then call done.
Build a simple to-do list: a text input, an 'Add' button, and a list below. Typing and pressing Add appends the item to the list (vanilla JavaScript). Single index.html. Then call done.

expect.label: the typed item appears in the list after Add

train.prompt: Build a Pomodoro focus timer showing 25:00, with Start and Reset buttons that run a working countdown in vanilla JavaScript. Single index.html. Then call done.

expect.label: the timer counts down after Start

train.prompt: Build a 3-tier pricing section: three cards (Basic, Pro, Team) each with a price, a few features, and a 'Choose' button. Single index.html. Then call done.

val.prompt: Build a simple calculator: a display and number/operator buttons that compute results in vanilla JavaScript. Single index.html. Then call done.
Build a live digital clock, centered, that updates every second with vanilla JavaScript and shows the current time. Single index.html. Then call done.

expect.label: the displayed time advances every second

val.prompt: Build a profile card: a circular avatar, a name, a short bio line, and a 'Follow' button. Single index.html. Then call done.

Build a tabbed panel: three tabs labeled 'Overview', 'Features', and 'Pricing', and below them a content area that shows ONLY the selected tab's paragraph. Clicking a tab reveals its panel and hides the others (vanilla JavaScript). Single index.html. Then call done.

expect.label: clicking Pricing changes the visible panel content

val.prompt: Build a text box with a live character counter below it that shows how many characters have been typed and updates as you type (vanilla JavaScript). Single index.html. Then call done.
